Stub functions for low power mode operations in a testing environment.

# Purpose
This code defines a set of functions related to low power management, likely intended for use in an embedded system or a device with power-saving capabilities. The functions [`low_power_standby`](<#low_power_standby>), [`low_power_enter`](<#low_power_enter>), and [`low_power_exit`](<#low_power_exit>) are declared but not implemented, indicating that they are placeholders for future functionality to manage different states of low power operation. The function [`low_power_is_active`](<#low_power_is_active>) returns a boolean value, currently set to `false`, which suggests it is meant to check if the system is in a low power state. This code provides narrow functionality focused on power management and is likely part of a larger system where these functions will be implemented to control power states.
